name: Route inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio 24/7

parameters.formTitle: Route inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio 24/7

parameters.message: =✅ Route inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Normalize Lead Payload

### Normalize Lead Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming lead records to a stable schema

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: inbound, outbound + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Delegate 'inbound' to Sub-workflow · ②→ Fast-Track 'outbound' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Delegate 'inbound' to Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Fast-Track 'outbound' + Alert

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Lead Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per lead (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Lead Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 25 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Guard Non-Empty email

### Guard Non-Empty email

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Sort Leads Newest First

### Sort Leads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

# Route inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio 24/7

**Pattern:** Lead Capture & Routing · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Route inbound leads with HubSpot and Twilio 24/7. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Normalize Lead Payload → Route by Work Type → Delegate 'inbound' to Sub-workflow → Merge Routed Work → Enrich with Reference Data → Fold In Enrichment Fields → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to lead capture & routing without manual effort
- Anyone automating routing in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; HubSpot private-app token; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
